latest.title: Total Voting Rights
latest.content:
Today 13:06
RNS Number : 8654Z
Videndum PLC
09 April 2026
9 April 2026
Videndum plc
Admission to Trading & TVR
Further to the announcement by Videndum plc (the "Company") on 8 April 2026 regarding Transactions by Directors and Persons Discharging Managerial Responsibilities (PDMRs), 171,116 New Ordinary Shares have been issued and allotted to PDMRs and Senior Managers, and are expected to be admitted to trading on 10 April 2026
.
The total issued ordinary share capital of the Company following admission will be 40,294,123 Ordinary Shares and the total number of voting rights of the Company will be 40,294,123. This figure may be used by Shareholders as the denominator for the calculations by which they will determine if they are required to notify their interest in, or a change in their interest in, the share capital of the Company under the FCA's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules.
Enquiries:
Jon Bolton
Group Company Secretary
020 8332 4600
A snapshot of Videndum plc
Videndum is a leading global provider of premium branded hardware products and software solutions to the growing content creation market. We are organised in three Divisions: Videndum Media Solutions, Videndum Production Solutions and Videndum Creative Solutions.
Videndum's customers include broadcasters, film studios, production and rental companies, photographers, independent content creators ("ICCs"), professional musicians and enterprises. Our product portfolio includes camera supports, video transmission systems and monitors, live streaming solutions, robotic camera systems, prompters, LED lighting, mobile power, carrying solutions, backgrounds, audio capture, and noise reduction equipment.
We employ around 1,250 people across the world in 9 different countries. Videndum plc is listed on the London Stock Exchange, ticker: VID.
More information can be found at:
https://videndum.com/
LEI number: 2138007H5DQ4X8YOCF14

8 April 2026
The following amendment has been made to the "Notification of Transactions by Directors and Persons Discharging Managerial Responsibilities (PDMRs)" announcement released on 8 April 2026 at 1:00pm under RNS No 6765Z.
The shares being subscribed for by Directors as part of the Refinancing are expected to be admitted to trading on 9 April 2026.
The notation of "PDMR" has been removed from Non-Executive Directors.
All other details remain unchanged, the full amended text is shown below.

latest.title: Directorate change
latest.content:
Today 14:30
RNS Number : 5045Z
Videndum PLC
07 April 2026
7 April 2026
Videndum plc
Board Changes
Videndum plc ("Videndum" or "the Company") announces that Martin Cooke will cease to be an independent non-executive director of the Company with effect from 30 April 2026. Martin was appointed at the end of July 2025 to support the Board with the refinancing of the business that successfully completed on 30 March 2026. At the same time, Graham Oldroyd will cease to be Deputy Chairman and will revert to his former role as Chair of the Finance Committee and an independent non-executive director.
Commenting on the Board changes, Stephen Harris, Videndum's Chairman said: "On behalf of the Board and our stakeholders I would like to express thanks to Martin who has provided invaluable support to enable Videndum to refinance its business and to secure its future in the most challenging of circumstances. Given that we now have a stable platform for growth the Board will be focusing on business opportunities and returning to a more common governance structure. Graham will revert to his previous role as an independent non-executive director and Chair of the Finance Committee. We are actively pursuing the recruitment of a permanent Chief Executive Officer and will update shareholders on progress with this search as soon as we have something able to."
For more information please contact:
Videndum plc
Stephen Harris, Chairman Email:
IR-enquiries@videndum.com
Brian Morgan, Group Chief Financial Officer
FTI Consulting
Richard Mountain
Telephone: 020 3272 1340
A snapshot of Videndum plc
Videndum is a leading global provider of premium branded hardware products and software solutions to the content creation market.
Our product portfolio includes camera supports, video transmission systems and monitors, live streaming solutions, robotic camera systems, prompters, LED lighting, mobile power, bags, backgrounds, audio capture, and noise reduction equipment.
We employ around 1,200 people across the world in 9 different countries. Videndum plc is listed on the London Stock Exchange, ticker: VID.
More information can be found at:
https://videndum.com/
LEI number: 2138007H5DQ4X8YOCF14

company_30s.what_they_do: The firm is a small‑cap listed entity with limited public disclosure about its core products.  The available filings suggest it operates in a niche segment of the financial services market, possibly offering investment or advisory services, but the precise business model is not clearly articulated.
company_30s.why_they_matter: Despite the opaque business description, the company has attracted attention because it has undergone a series of governance and capital‑raising moves that could signal a strategic pivot or an attempt to stabilize cash flow.  These actions hint at an underlying business that may be trying to reposition itself in a crowded market.
company_30s.current_state: The company is in a restructuring phase.  New directors have been appointed, share‑holding corrections have been filed, and a significant capital‑raising programme has been launched.  The lack of clear revenue figures and the recent dilution concerns suggest that the firm is still struggling to achieve sustainable profitability, but the influx of fresh capital may provide breathing room if the business can realign its operations.

rns_narrative: The company has focused on two main fronts: first, a sweeping governance overhaul with director changes and share‑holding corrections; second, a comprehensive capital‑raising and refinancing package that included an open offer and firm placing.  These moves appear aimed at restoring investor confidence and securing the liquidity needed for future growth.

The annual results released in March provide a baseline of performance, but the filings also highlight the company’s need to raise additional funds to cover operating costs.  The narrative is one of a firm in survival mode, looking to turn a deep decline into a new growth trajectory.

If the capital raise succeeds and the company can demonstrate a clear path to profitability, the market could see a turnaround.  However, the absence of a well‑defined product line remains a key uncertainty.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Skeptical silence with occasional criticism of dilution
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: No clear bullish chatter; the few supportive posts are tied to the hope that the capital raise will stabilize the firm.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Critics point to the severe dilution and lack of a proven business model, warning that the company could slip further if the new funding fails to translate into earnings.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The community remains largely quiet; sporadic posts are negative, but there is no sustained rallying cry.  The mood is one of cautious observation rather than active engagement.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is flat, indicating no public awareness.  A sudden news event could therefore generate a sharp, unexpected reaction.

near_term_catalysts: Completion of the open offer and the firm placing, which will bring in fresh equity and potentially reduce leverage.
The next quarterly earnings report could reveal whether the new capital is being deployed effectively.
Any announcement of a new product line, partnership, or regulatory approval that clarifies the company’s revenue stream.
A volume spike that breaks the current support zone, signaling renewed institutional interest.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a clear price breakout above the recent low, accompanied by a 20‑day moving‑average crossover and a surge in trading volume.  Positive guidance or a revised profit outlook would also be a strong confirmation.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the early stages of a post‑crash recovery.  The moderate compression score suggests the market is still testing the waters.  A sustained rally would need to be supported by tangible financial metrics and a credible business plan.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The firm has experienced a severe decline, creating a large upside window if the capital raise is successful.  Historically, deep crashes in the 70‑95% range have produced several hundred percent recoveries when the catalyst arrives.  If the price returns to the midpoint of its 52‑week range, the upside could easily exceed 200% from the current level.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The company’s business model is unclear and it has suffered significant dilution.  Without a clear path to profitability, the risk of a further slide remains.  The worst‑case scenario is a continued decline to the low end of its 52‑week range, potentially erasing the investment.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential loss: 10–15% of the invested capital if the price stays flat or falls slightly.  Potential gain: 200–300% if the stock rebounds to the upper half of its historical range.  The asymmetry is strong – a modest downside versus a very large upside.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter after a confirmed breakout above the recent low with volume exceeding the 20‑day average.  Use a position size that limits exposure to 10% of the portfolio, and set a stop 12% below the entry to protect against a false breakout.
playbook.if_watching: Hold cash until a clear sign of institutional accumulation appears – a sudden spike in volume coupled with a break of the 20‑day moving average.  Alternatively, wait for the next earnings release that shows a positive revenue trend.
playbook.if_passing: If the company files for bankruptcy or the capital raise fails to close, consider exiting the position immediately.

ai_insights.bottom_line: A deep, poorly‑understood decline has created a high‑reward, low‑downside play – if the capital raise materialises and the firm can articulate a clear revenue path, the upside is substantial; if not, the risk of a further slide remains.
